API keys are stored in Keychain. Saving: API keys are stored in Keychain. Saving or using a saved key may ask you to allow ArchiCode to access it.
App-global preferences that apply across: App-global preferences that apply across every project.

app.languageHint: Choose the language used by ArchiCode. More languages can be added without changing the UI code.
Applies to implementation/build/debug sh: Applies to implementation/build/debug shell commands such as setup, verification, run-target, and finite console commands. Planning review, source-change review, and Research chat approvals use separate toggles. High-risk commands still pause for manual approval even when this is enabled.
Approve the selected run in the Runs pan: Approve the selected run in the Runs panel.

Archi will help turn it into a clear nex: Archi will help turn it into a clear next step.
ArchiCode adjusts compaction and context: ArchiCode adjusts compaction and context selection from the active provider/model. Unknown custom models use conservative limits.
ArchiCode applied the smallest validated: ArchiCode applied the smallest validated patch to the existing map.
ArchiCode couldn't read or update this p: ArchiCode couldn't read or update this project's.gitattributes file: {{value1}}. Fix the file (content or permissions) and reload the project to set up merge-friendly storage.
ArchiCode found a conflicting.gitattribu: ArchiCode found a conflicting.gitattributes rule and left it unchanged.
ArchiCode generated {{value1}} architect: ArchiCode generated {{value1}} architecture flows and completed the selected review. No action is required.
ArchiCode is a visual-first harness for: ArchiCode is a visual-first harness for designing and evolving software projects with node-based architecture diagrams and LLM-guided workflows. It is meant to make the diagram, not a long chat thread, the durable planning surface for a project.
ArchiCode is applying a conservative, ev: ArchiCode is applying a conservative, evidence-backed update.
ArchiCode is looking for the latest publ: ArchiCode is looking for the latest public version.
ArchiCode is preparing context before th: ArchiCode is preparing context before this run starts.
ArchiCode loads every existing instructi: ArchiCode loads every existing instruction file into both Chat and Build context, regardless of the selected LLM provider. Put shared guidance in
ArchiCode only undoes node movement, lay: ArchiCode only undoes node movement, layout, size, shape, and color. Semantic graph and node-property changes remain protected from realtime undo; use Git when you need to revert them.
ArchiCode opens project folders with thi: ArchiCode opens project folders with this app.
ArchiCode will forget its saved informat: ArchiCode will forget its saved information for this project, including the project map, nodes and flows, notes, run records, chat history, and ArchiCode settings. Your source code and regular project files will stay untouched. Opening this folder in ArchiCode later will require importing it again to rebuild the map.
ArchiCode will persist the command, deci: ArchiCode will persist the command, decision, logs, exit code, and run instructions as JSON.
Architecture concepts in the selected pe: Architecture concepts in the selected perspective
Architecture nodes, notes, and rules ava: Architecture nodes, notes, and rules available for graph-to-graph semantic context.

are symbols. Lines represent containment: are symbols. Lines represent containment, imports, resolved calls, or runtime links.

Ask the chat agent to find contradiction: Ask the chat agent to find contradictions, missing information, and illogical nodes, edges, or flow boundaries.

Attach reusable guidance for agents work: Attach reusable guidance for agents working from this node.

Auto uses detected model metadata or con: Auto uses detected model metadata or conservative known-model defaults. Suggestions are editable because provider catalogs change.
Auto-approve commands allowed by filesys: Auto-approve commands allowed by filesystem policy
Automatically applies non-destructive gr: Automatically applies non-destructive graph changes and safety-classified medium-risk Chat commands across this project. High-risk actions, project-code edits, deletes, and run actions still require review or their dedicated workflow.
Automatically deletes resolved node note: Automatically deletes resolved node notes after approving a node, keeping only open notes and active questions.
Automatically reads new assistant messag: Automatically reads new assistant messages aloud after they finish generating.
Available after this project is committe: Available after this project is committed to Git
Available when OpenAI Realtime is select: Available when OpenAI Realtime is selected.
Balanced default for both light and dark: Balanced default for both light and dark themes.

Both models are bundled. Changing this m: Both models are bundled. Changing this machine-local preference clears incompatible vectors and rebuilds the active project's index.

Browser preview provider check is simula: Browser preview provider check is simulated.
Build is paused so the LLM does not code: Build is paused so the LLM does not code from missing requirements. Continue only when you intentionally want it to proceed with current assumptions.
call in a temporary Kimi home. The tempo: call in a temporary Kimi home. The temporary session is removed afterward; the signed-in credential store remains shared.

for local Claude runs. ArchiCode still s: for local Claude runs. ArchiCode still saves runs and artifacts, but Claude should not reuse or save its own CLI session state.

for this feature. Acceptance criteria wi: for this feature. Acceptance criteria will be kept.

Incrementally verify repository changes: Incrementally verify repository changes and apply the smallest safe patch to the existing map.

Keep one concrete, verifiable outcome pe: Keep one concrete, verifiable outcome per line.
Keep the current routed edges or switch: Keep the current routed edges or switch to curved edges across the graph.
Kimi print mode is autonomous, so ArchiC: Kimi print mode is autonomous, so ArchiCode injects first-match static deny rules in an isolated Kimi home. Read-only blocks edits and shell tools; workspace write allows project Write/Edit but leaves shell, delegated agents, and MCP denied. Full access falls back to the user's Kimi rules.
Label: Label
Large reports and working files created: Large reports and working files created for this chat are loaded only when requested.
Lets chat and build/debug runs delegate: Lets chat and build/debug runs delegate bounded test, visual, runtime, and emulator audits. Delphi can approval-gated start an existing Run App profile, wait for it, and stop only what it started; missing tools use a separate setup card.
Lets chat and run agents delegate read-o: Lets chat and run agents delegate read-only graph assessment plus detailed graph design, refinement, and reconciliation. Picasso never applies changes directly; proposed changes require review and may follow a successful Solomon run.
Lets chat and run agents delegate substa: Lets chat and run agents delegate substantial read-only codebase, online, or topic research to an isolated evidence-focused subagent.
Lets implementation runs execute bounded: Lets implementation runs execute bounded commands through ArchiCode's console guard when command approval and filesystem policy allow it.
Lets implementation runs inspect previou: Lets implementation runs inspect previous run records, plans, diffs, logs, and saved artifacts for context.
Lets implementation runs use built-in to: Lets implementation runs use built-in tools to list, search, and read files inside the project before making decisions.
Lets Research chat ask approval to run S: Lets Research chat ask approval to run Solomon for git merge conflicts. Solomon reads conflicted files, writes the approved resolution, and verifies it.
Limit matching relationships to node typ: Limit matching relationships to node types such as service, database, feature, or task. Leave empty to match any node type.
Limit the rule to named imports such as: Limit the rule to named imports such as readFile or exec. Leave empty to forbid the whole package or module.
Limit where ArchiCode-run commands can w: Limit where ArchiCode-run commands can work.
Listed for debugging. Leave these flags: Listed for debugging. Leave these flags as they are unless you are sure the harness inferred them incorrectly.

Local meaning-based retrieval for codeba: Local meaning-based retrieval for codebase imports, research chat, and AI build context.

Local Streamable HTTP endpoint for exter: Local Streamable HTTP endpoint for external Codex and Claude Code clients.
Local text-to-speech uses a downloadable: Local text-to-speech uses a downloadable Transformers.js Kokoro 82M q8 model.
Local-only advisory matches from the sem: Local-only advisory matches from the semantic cache. They are never saved into the graph or committed to Git.

No implementation hints are available fo: No implementation hints are available for this node.
No indexed source-code matches cleared t: No indexed source-code matches cleared the similarity threshold for this node.
No matching subject identity appears in: No matching subject identity appears in another flow.
No nodes with acceptance criteria in thi: No nodes with acceptance criteria in this flow
No registry servers matched this filter: No registry servers matched this filter yet.
No registry servers matched this search: No registry servers matched this search yet.
No related implementation has been recor: No related implementation has been recorded for this node.
No reusable command approvals saved yet.
No safe presentation change to undo: No safe presentation change to undo
No source code is indexed yet. Rebuild t: No source code is indexed yet. Rebuild the Semantic Code Index in Settings → Advanced.
No source location was retained for this: No source location was retained for this aggregated relationship.
No source-change artifact was recorded f: No source-change artifact was recorded for this manual code review. Reject with guidance or cancel this run.
No strong graph-node matches are cached.
No tests yet. Write acceptance criteria: No tests yet. Write acceptance criteria above, then Generate tests.
No tracked Git diff is available for thi: No tracked Git diff is available for this file.
No-change runs skip parsing, graph mutat: No-change runs skip parsing, graph mutation, and LLM review.
Node and edge IDs, wording, layout, flow: Node and edge IDs, wording, layout, flow membership, notes, and custom properties remain untouched when evidence is unchanged.
Node-scoped work items used by the harne: Node-scoped work items used by the harness and LLM runs.
Nothing to preview — this card was alrea: Nothing to preview — this card was already reviewed
On macOS, API keys are stored in Keychai: On macOS, API keys are stored in Keychain. Saving or using a saved key may ask you to allow ArchiCode to access it.
On macOS, Brave API keys are stored in K: On macOS, Brave API keys are stored in Keychain. Saving or using a saved key may ask you to allow ArchiCode to access it.
One argument per line for stdio servers.
Only local branches are shown. Branch ch: Only local branches are shown. Branch changes reload the project data from disk. Merge, rebase, resolve conflicts, and manage remotes in your Git client or terminal.
Only selected flows may change; other fl: Only selected flows may change; other flows keep pending code changes for later.
Open a folder or create a project to ins: Open a folder or create a project to inspect nodes, runs, notes, and artifacts.
Open a folder, clone from Git, or choose: Open a folder, clone from Git, or choose a template.
Open a local codebase, clone a Git repos: Open a local codebase, clone a Git repository, or start from a template. Configure an LLM provider when you want ArchiCode to map and reason across the project.
Open a project folder or create a projec: Open a project folder or create a project from a template.
Open a project to start scoped research.
Open acceptance criteria in large editor: Open acceptance criteria in large editor
Open Activity > Errors or the latest run: Open Activity > Errors or the latest run details for this node.
Open Code Knowledge Map for project-wide: Open Code Knowledge Map for project-wide files, symbols, imports, calls, and runtime links.
Open or close the research chat panel.
Open the add-node context menu at the la: Open the add-node context menu at the last pointer position over empty canvas. Universal shortcut.
Open the current project folder in {{sel: Open the current project folder in {{selectedCodeIdeLabel}}.
Open the Git status / commit / push / pu: Open the Git status / commit / push / pull dialog.
Open the note composer. Nothing is saved: Open the note composer. Nothing is saved until you add the note.
Open the project-wide file, symbol, impo: Open the project-wide file, symbol, import, call, and runtime knowledge map.
Open the Questions tab to answer blocker: Open the Questions tab to answer blockers before the plan can continue.
OpenAI Realtime uses the separately save: OpenAI Realtime uses the separately saved OpenAI API key and is independent of the normal chat provider.
OpenCode runs once per request. Read-onl: OpenCode runs once per request. Read-only phases receive explicit edit, shell, and external-directory denies; write-capable build phases use --auto.
OpenCode runs once per request. Write-ca: OpenCode runs once per request. Write-capable build phases add --auto; use OpenCode permissions/config for finer-grained tool restrictions.
Opened projects will show up here.
Optional absolute paths, one per line: Optional absolute paths, one per line
Optional guidance for the next run: Optional guidance for the next run
Optional reason to show the provider whe: Optional reason to show the provider when this tool call is denied
Optionally require the file name, before: Optionally require the file name, before its extension, to follow a common naming style.
Other indexed code with similar meaning: Other indexed code with similar meaning
Overrides Codex model_verbosity for each: Overrides Codex model_verbosity for each ArchiCode invocation without changing Codex files.
Pandora will not start, and no project s: Pandora will not start, and no project source files will be changed.
Paste a node from the clipboard. Univers: Paste a node from the clipboard. Universal shortcut.
Paste the URL above, leave bearer token: Paste the URL above, leave bearer token env var empty, then add direct headers.
Percentages are local embedding similari: Percentages are local embedding similarity scores. They suggest relevance; they do not prove ownership or dependency.
Pick any local folder to open in ArchiCo: Pick any local folder to open in ArchiCode.
Pin this note as durable context for thi: Pin this note as durable context for this node.
Planning and implementation remain indep: Planning and implementation remain independently configurable phases of the same AI Implement agent.
Planning chooses Fast or High based on t: Planning chooses Fast or High based on the requested scope, risk, and expected implementation horizon.
Prevents AI Build, Run, Debug, command i: Prevents AI Build, Run, Debug, command inference, and runtime-profile reconciliation from changing the configured build command or run targets. You can still edit them manually here.
Preview this proposed flow. It has not b: Preview this proposed flow. It has not been added to the project yet.
Project root is always allowed. Full-acc: Project root is always allowed. Full-access disables root checks and should be temporary.
Project-wide files, symbols, and structu: Project-wide files, symbols, and structural relationships
Proposed changes from a pending graph ed: Proposed changes from a pending graph edit — not yet applied.
Proposed for removal by a pending graph: Proposed for removal by a pending graph edit — not yet applied.
Proposed new node from a pending graph e: Proposed new node from a pending graph edit — not yet created.
Protects the write-capable local endpoin: Protects the write-capable local endpoint from other local processes that do not have the generated token.
Provider activity, safeguards, coverage: Provider activity, safeguards, coverage notes, and phase timing
Provider cards are saved on this compute: Provider cards are saved on this computer and reused across projects. The enabled card supplies the default model for chat and build agents unless a chat selection or LLM Profile overrides it.
Provider, command, context, web search,: Provider, command, context, web search, and permission settings. Context is assembled automatically from the loaded project model.
Purge resolved node notes when a node is: Purge resolved node notes when a node is approved
Approving starts an AI implementation ru: Approving starts an AI implementation run for the selected scope. It can create or modify project files, and its progress and results will appear in Activity.
Quiet, denser gray with stronger contras: Quiet, denser gray with stronger contrast.
Re-read the project model from disk.
Re-scan local source and verify whether: Re-scan local source and verify whether imported relationships are still supported.
Re-scan the project and rebuild local fi: Re-scan the project and rebuild local file, symbol, dependency, call, and runtime evidence.
Read-only 3D flow visualization. Use WAS: Read-only 3D flow visualization. Use WASD keys and mouse drag to navigate.
Read-only snapshots from the current bra: Read-only snapshots from the current branch’s Git history
Readable node metadata. The full JSON st: Readable node metadata. The full JSON still lives in the project files for LLM clarity.
Rearrange all nodes in the current flow: Rearrange all nodes in the current flow into a grid.
Rearrange the visible nodes in this flow: Rearrange the visible nodes in this flow or subflow. This only changes canvas positions.
Rearrange the visible nodes into a clean: Rearrange the visible nodes into a clean layout.
Recent commit messages and Git operation: Recent commit messages and Git operation output will appear here.
Records the violation as useful architec: Records the violation as useful architecture information. It is visible but never blocks a run.
Refresh evidence first to build the proj: Refresh evidence first to build the project-wide code knowledge map.
Reject the selected run in the Runs pane: Reject the selected run in the Runs panel.
Reject this node's current draft state a: Reject this node's current draft state and keep it editable.
Reload ArchiCode's project JSON from dis: Reload ArchiCode's project JSON from disk, useful after external edits or stale state.
Remember approval for this command and w: Remember approval for this command and working folder
Remove staged attachments from this draf: Remove staged attachments from this draft note. No files are deleted.
Remove the generated acceptance tests fo: Remove the generated acceptance tests for this node.
Remove this report from the open bug lis: Remove this report from the open bug list. This does not run Pandora or delete project files.
Removed runs are hidden from the queue.
Removed runs are hidden. Use the eye but: Removed runs are hidden. Use the eye button to view history.
Removes ArchiCode's saved project inform: Removes ArchiCode's saved project information, but not your source files. Reopening this folder in ArchiCode will require a new import.
Reopen the report from this project's on: Reopen the report from this project's one-time initial codebase import.
Reopen this node for changes and remove: Reopen this node for changes and remove its approval lock.
Reopen this note so it becomes active co: Reopen this note so it becomes active context again.
Report a bug or unexpected behavior rela: Report a bug or unexpected behavior related to this node
Reports any dependency from selected sou: Reports any dependency from selected source files to forbidden target files.
Reports dependencies from selected sourc: Reports dependencies from selected source files to anything outside the allowed target areas. This can enforce layer direction and public module boundaries.
Reports each selected source file that d: Reports each selected source file that does not depend on at least one required target.
Reports imports of selected packages, mo: Reports imports of selected packages, modules, or named imported APIs.
Reports matching violations in the graph: Reports matching violations in the graph and issue panel, but never fails a run.
Reports selected graph nodes that have n: Reports selected graph nodes that have no incoming or outgoing relationship.
Repository evidence matches the previous: Repository evidence matches the previous baseline. The graph was not regenerated or rewritten.
Require a matching relationship or repor: Require a matching relationship or report every matching relationship as forbidden.
Require answers before manual plan appro: Require answers before manual plan approval
Required test, documentation, or compani: Required test, documentation, or companion patterns
Requires each selected source file to ha: Requires each selected source file to have a matching test, documentation, or other companion file.
Requires graph nodes to contain acceptan: Requires graph nodes to contain acceptance criteria, checks, documentation, implementation scope, or other structured metadata.
Requires or forbids incoming or outgoing: Requires or forbids incoming or outgoing relationships between selected graph nodes and optional node types.
research.reviewOutcomePreserved: I kept this exact review outcome and did not generate another proposal.
research.reviewNoAutomaticRetry: No automatic retry was created. Ask explicitly for a retry or a new proposal if you want to change the remaining operations.
research.reviewRejectedReason: Rejected or left unapplied by the user.
Reserved by the browser or window manage: Reserved by the browser or window manager. Choose a different chord.
Reset this binding to its default: Reset this binding to its default
Resolved graph-change records older than: Resolved graph-change records older than this are moved to a cold archive file and dropped from the hot ledger. Pending changes are always kept.
Restart ArchiCode to load Git stash supp: Restart ArchiCode to load Git stash support in this app window.
Restart ArchiCode to load the local Git: Restart ArchiCode to load the local Git initialization bridge for this app window.
Restore default panel sizes and viewport: Restore default panel sizes and viewport for the current project.
Restore flow to agent working set: Restore flow to agent working set
Restore subflow to agent working set: Restore subflow to agent working set
Restore the default panel sizes, docking: Restore the default panel sizes, docking, selected workbench view, activity tab, and canvas viewport.
Resync compares the architecture graph w: Resync compares the architecture graph with current code. Ignore clears this notice without changing the graph.
Resync every flow and advance the projec: Resync every flow and advance the project-wide evidence baseline.
Retry the run, or start a debug pass wit: Retry the run, or start a debug pass with the failure context.
Retry the selected failed run in the Run: Retry the selected failed run in the Runs panel.
Return to the complete map at its defaul: Return to the complete map at its default zoom.
Return to the selected architecture lens: Return to the selected architecture lens.
Reusable advice included in agent contex: Reusable advice included in agent context. It guides implementation but is not checked against the code automatically.
Review every flow together, including du: Review every flow together, including duplicated responsibilities, conflicting assumptions, and unclear cross-flow handoffs.
Review files changed since graph analysi: Review files changed since graph analysis
Review is required before the run can co: Review is required before the run can continue.
Review model-proposed graph edits such a: Review model-proposed graph edits such as node text, edges, new nodes, and subflows. Source code edits are reviewed in Source Changes.
Review questions, logs, artifacts, diffs: Review questions, logs, artifacts, diffs, and proposed changes before treating work as done.
Review the generated source changes, the: Review the generated source changes, then approve the run to continue verification.
Review the selected node and add missing: Review the selected node and add missing specification details without splitting it
Review this exact command, then approve: Review this exact command, then approve or reject it.
Review, edit, select, or resolve reporte: Review, edit, select, or resolve reported bugs before asking Pandora to fix them.
Run activity will appear here for runs s: Run activity will appear here for runs still shown in the queue.
Run AI Implement with Gaia, Build, Run A: Run AI Implement with Gaia, Build, Run App, or AI Debug with Pandora from the toolbar when the graph has enough context.
Run an AI agent that writes real (failin: Run an AI agent that writes real (failing) test files for these criteria and syncs this list to the criteria
Run an AI agent that writes real test fi: Run an AI agent that writes real test files from acceptance criteria for every eligible node in this flow
Run builds or agent jobs to create logs,: Run builds or agent jobs to create logs, summaries, diffs, and instructions.
Run every test and update its status (gr: Run every test and update its status (green/red) on this node
Runs will appear here with live logs.
Same file name requires each source and: Same file name requires each source and companion to share a base name. Any matching file only checks that at least one companion exists.
Save settings after changing the toggle. Import and AI features fall back to structural context if this service is unavailable.
Save the current flow or subflow scope a: Save the current flow or subflow scope as a draw.io / diagrams.net XML file.
Save the full loaded ArchiCode metadata: Save the full loaded ArchiCode metadata bundle as one JSON file.
Save this draft as a node note. Staged r: Save this draft as a node note. Staged references are attached only after this click.
Save this report's edited title, descrip: Save this report's edited title, description, and priority without starting Pandora.
Saved key will be used. Paste a new key: Saved key will be used. Paste a new key here to replace it.
Saved locally on this computer and hidde: Saved locally on this computer and hidden from project JSON.
Search concepts, communities, relationsh: Search concepts, communities, relationships, or source paths…
Search files, symbols, languages, or com: Search files, symbols, languages, or communities…
Search includes concept names, descripti: Search includes concept names, descriptions, community names, relationship types, and source evidence.
Search matches file paths, symbol names,: Search matches file paths, symbol names, languages, kinds, and community names.
Search or create a node type: Search or create a node type
Select an item to open source, trace imp: Select an item to open source, trace impact, or start a path.
Select an LLM provider to edit phase pol: Select an LLM provider to edit phase policies.
Select at least one flow to continue.
Select one or more nodes to run AI Imple: Select one or more nodes to run AI Implement with node scope.
Select one target or combine several, su: Select one target or combine several, such as a backend and frontend in a monorepo.
Sent as text.verbosity for GPT-5.6 Respo: Sent as text.verbosity for GPT-5.6 Responses API requests. Other models and Chat Completions are unchanged.
Show or hide the bottom activity panel.
Show or hide the canvas minimap. Univers: Show or hide the canvas minimap. Universal shortcut.
Show or hide the runtime services grid i: Show or hide the runtime services grid in the right sidebar.
Show run, artifact, and advanced tabs: Show run, artifact, and advanced tabs
Show system notifications when jobs fini: Show system notifications when jobs finish
Show system notifications when reviews n: Show system notifications when reviews need attention
Show the full saved trace again: Show the full saved trace again
Show this flow's top-level nodes. Drop a: Show this flow's top-level nodes. Drop a subflow here to move it to top level. Double-click to rename.
Showing {{length}} of {{length2}} matche: Showing {{length}} of {{length2}} matches. Keep typing to narrow the list.
Showing a safe local cap of {{length}} n: Showing a safe local cap of {{length}} nodes and {{length2}} edges.
Shown in the sidebar, exported bundles,: Shown in the sidebar, exported bundles, context, and run handoffs.
Shows speaker actions on assistant messa: Shows speaker actions on assistant messages and allows local text-to-speech playback.
Shows the microphone action in scoped re: Shows the microphone action in scoped research chat and allows local speech transcription.
Shows the same stable subject identity w: Shows the same stable subject identity when it appears in another flow. This is not semantic matching, code ownership, or a Leiden community.
Small, important knowledge retained for: Small, important knowledge retained for this project. Notes are local and may be scoped to a flow, subflow, or node.
Soft blue-gray for a lighter planning su: Soft blue-gray for a lighter planning surface.
Solid lines are extracted or resolved; d: Solid lines are extracted or resolved; dashed lines are inferred.
Some files still have unresolved conflic: Some files still have unresolved conflicts (marked below). Resolve them in your Git client, terminal, or ask Solomon — Merge Arbiter through Research chat, then commit here to finish the merge.
Stage tracks where this node sits in the: Stage tracks where this node sits in the workflow. Agents usually update it as work moves forward, unless you want to override the lifecycle manually.
Start a debug pass from this failure.
Start a new research chat scoped to this: Start a new research chat scoped to this node's context
Start a scoped chat and it will appear h: Start a scoped chat and it will appear here.
Starts a local MCP endpoint after saving: Starts a local MCP endpoint after saving settings. External clients with the token can read and apply validated graph changes.
Stash these changes before switching to: Stash these changes before switching to {{pendingStashBranch}}.
Stored by ArchiCode in the main process: Stored by ArchiCode in the main process and used only to mint short-lived Realtime client credentials.
Stored for {{label}}. Provider default i: Stored for {{label}}. Provider default inherits its current model {{value2}}.
Stored in ArchiCode application data, ne: Stored in ArchiCode application data, never in the project repository.
Stored only in this project's local Arch: Stored only in this project's local ArchiCode state. Regenerating restarts the hosted endpoint if it is running.
Subject: Subject
Submit the composer message. Adding any: Submit the composer message. Adding any modifier inserts a newline instead. Universal shortcut.
Subtle blue canvas without turning the a: Subtle blue canvas without turning the app monochrome.
Superseded by a newer proposal — preview: Superseded by a newer proposal — preview unavailable
Supporting model calls that are not stan: Supporting model calls that are not standalone agents.
Switch between the 2D flow editor and th: Switch between the 2D flow editor and the read-only 3D view.
Switch the activity panel to the Artifac: Switch the activity panel to the Artifacts tab.
Switch the activity panel to the Errors: Switch the activity panel to the Errors tab.
Switch the activity panel to the Git tab: Switch the activity panel to the Git tab.
Switch the activity panel to the Plan ta: Switch the activity panel to the Plan tab.
Switch the activity panel to the Questio: Switch the activity panel to the Questions tab.
Switch the activity panel to the Runs ta: Switch the activity panel to the Runs tab.
Switch the activity panel to the Source: Switch the activity panel to the Source Changes tab.
Switch the activity panel to the Trace t: Switch the activity panel to the Trace tab.
Switch the right sidebar to the Properti: Switch the right sidebar to the Properties tab.
Switch the workbench between file browse: Switch the workbench between file browser and flow canvas.
Switch to All to browse recent chats fro: Switch to All to browse recent chats from the project.
Tag this node in the active research cha: Tag this node in the active research chat so you can ask questions about it
Tell LLM agents which console shell to a: Tell LLM agents which console shell to assume for commands and examples.
Tell the next retry what must change. Re: Tell the next retry what must change. Reject stops this run; cancel stops it without a review decision.
Tests derived from the acceptance criter: Tests derived from the acceptance criteria above. Green = passing, red = failing, grey = not run yet. Generate tests syncs this list to the criteria and writes the test files; Run all tests executes them and updates status. Hover a row to see which criterion it verifies.
Text that must appear at the end of the: Text that must appear at the end of the file name before its extension, such as.service or Controller.
The absolute folder loaded on this machi: The absolute folder loaded on this machine. This is not written to shareable project.json.
The agent asked for input before coding. Resolve these node notes to keep the build grounded.
The agent has a question that needs an a: The agent has a question that needs an answer. Check Notes.
The agent produced a plan for this node: The agent produced a plan for this node and is waiting for you to review it.
The agent produced code changes for this: The agent produced code changes for this node and is waiting for you to review them.
The AI Debug agent for focused failure i: The AI Debug agent for focused failure investigation, repair, and recovery.
The archived chat remains stored with th: The archived chat remains stored with the project and is not permanently deleted.
The assistant tried to use this Ask-mode: The assistant tried to use this Ask-mode MCP tool. Approve or reject this tool use.
The chat will no longer appear in All or: The chat will no longer appear in All or Scope history.
The Code Knowledge Map and enabled seman: The Code Knowledge Map and enabled semantic index are current. The architecture graph was left unchanged.
The current graph remains the source of: The current graph remains the source of truth. ArchiCode hashes the repository, freezes unchanged entities, and reconciles only the affected evidence and relationships.
The exact local rule that will be evalua: The exact local rule that will be evaluated continuously. Hover each option for its behavior and required inputs.
The exact paths were unavailable for thi: The exact paths were unavailable for this older warning.
The full snapshot remains local and agen: The full snapshot remains local and agents query only bounded results.
The hosted MCP path is bound to 127.0.0.1 only.
The import is finished. Review the resul: The import is finished. Review the result, then open the generated map.
The intended rhythm is: model the system: The intended rhythm is: model the system visually, clarify uncertainty on the relevant nodes, run focused agent work, verify the result, then keep the graph updated as the shared source of project intent. If code changed outside ArchiCode, ask Archi to sync graph to code; it should first ask whether to compare uncommitted changes, changes since a commit you name, or the full codebase.
The last run is paused waiting for you t: The last run is paused waiting for you to approve a command or permission request.
The main chat agent. A model selected in: The main chat agent. A model selected inside an individual chat overrides this default for that chat.
The structured node information that mus: The structured node information that must be present for every node in scope.
The verified patch was applied; ambiguou: The verified patch was applied; ambiguous user-owned items were preserved for your review.
These reflect your current key bindings. Customize them in Preferences:
This approval applies only to this exact: This approval applies only to this exact change. It cannot be remembered for later rule edits.
This can take a moment. You can keep rev: This can take a moment. You can keep reviewing the chat while ArchiCode finishes.
This check runs locally from repository: This check runs locally from repository and graph facts. It adds no AI-model latency or token cost.
This clears the acceptance-check list fo: This clears the acceptance-check list for this node and deletes generated files from
This copy should be updated from Microso: This copy should be updated from Microsoft Store. GitHub downloads are meant for non-Store desktop installs.
This file is binary and cannot be displa: This file is binary and cannot be displayed as text.
This file no longer exists and no workin: This file no longer exists and no working-tree Git diff is available.
This folder did not have ArchiCode graph: This folder did not have ArchiCode graph metadata yet. ArchiCode created an empty workspace; you can let an LLM map the current codebase into flows and nodes.
This initial import is a one-time operat: This initial import is a one-time operation and can take time while ArchiCode analyzes and reviews the codebase. Skipping is allowed, but the graph will remain empty until you generate a map or add nodes manually.
This is a non-AI offline mode for using: This is a non-AI offline mode for using ArchiCode as a living diagram, run ledger, artifact browser, and permissioned command shell. It cannot plan or code with an LLM until another provider is selected.
This map covers the project rather than: This map covers the project rather than only the selected architecture lens.
This node has an open agent question. Wr: This node has an open agent question. Write the answer naturally; the agent will read it with the rest of the node context.
This node has changed planning state. Re: This node has changed planning state. Requires implementation.
This node has source changes that have n: This node has source changes that have not passed a build/test/check command yet. Use AI Run > Build.
This node's draft was rejected and needs: This node's draft was rejected and needs revision before it can move forward.
This policy is not attached to a graph n: This policy is not attached to a graph node.
This project already has a conflicting.g: This project already has a conflicting.gitattributes rule for ArchiCode graph history. ArchiCode left it unchanged.
This project does not have a saved repor: This project does not have a saved report from its initial codebase import.
This removes the node data and any conne: This removes the node data and any connected edges from the current flow.
This run is waiting for its turn.
This run was stopped before completion.
This runs an AI agent that writes real t: This runs an AI agent that writes real test files for the
This starts a read-only chat review. It: This starts a read-only chat review. It will not edit the graph, change source files, or queue a run.
This tells ArchiCode which runnable part: This tells ArchiCode which runnable part of the project this node belongs to when it needs to build, run, or test work for this feature. In a single-target project, Auto usually just works. In a multi-target project, Auto leaves it open until Gaia's AI Implement run can confidently map the node once. Choose None to opt out, or pick a specific module to lock the node to that target manually.
Time remaining before Archi Live reconne: Time remaining before Archi Live reconnects this chat
Title: Title
Tokenizer-safe chunks spanning the full: Tokenizer-safe chunks spanning the full eligible source, grouped by functions, classes, methods, and components.
Trace cleared for this run. New activity: Trace cleared for this run. New activity will appear here.
Transformers.js Whisper does not auto-de: Transformers.js Whisper does not auto-detect language yet; choose the spoken language for multilingual input.
Try a different search or add a node to: Try a different search or add a node to this scope.
Undo the latest safe node movement, layo: Undo the latest safe node movement, layout, size, shape, or color change. Universal shortcut.
Unpin this note so it no longer stays at: Unpin this note so it no longer stays at the top of relevant notes.
Update checks are available in the packa: Update checks are available in the packaged Electron app.
Update the existing architecture map fro: Update the existing architecture map from verified code changes while preserving truthful graph and user-authored content.
Use 1.0 for natural speed; Kokoro suppor: Use 1.0 for natural speed; Kokoro supports a small speed adjustment range.
Use AI Implement to ask Gaia for a node-: Use AI Implement to ask Gaia for a node-scoped run.
Use another port if the default is alrea: Use another port if the default is already in use.
Use ArchiCode's built-in Brave Search to: Use ArchiCode's built-in Brave Search tool so any provider can search through the same backend.
Use provider-native web search when the: Use provider-native web search when the selected model/endpoint supports it. Models without native search stay web-disabled except for direct URL fetches and any separately configured MCP tools.
Use Settings - MCP servers - Connect to: Use Settings - MCP servers - Connect to a custom MCP.
Use short questions, answers, decisions,: Use short questions, answers, decisions, and references. This is shared context with the AI Agent, not a chat transcript.
Use the larger workspace for a clear, de: Use the larger workspace for a clear, detailed description.
Use this when the work may touch multipl: Use this when the work may touch multiple flows, modules, or graph areas.
Use this when the work should stay cente: Use this when the work should stay centered on the current flow, with only related supporting changes outside it.
Use this when the work should stay centered on the selected node set, while still allowing the agent to inspect nearby context.
Used by direct OpenAI Realtime WebRTC se: Used by direct OpenAI Realtime WebRTC sessions.
Used by the toolbar Build button and by: Used by the toolbar Build button and by AI runs when they verify generated changes.
Used only for live audio sessions, not n: Used only for live audio sessions, not normal chat responses.
User-edited or ambiguous content is pres: User-edited or ambiguous content is preserved and reported as an actionable conflict.
Uses the bundled CPU embedding model to: Uses the bundled CPU embedding model to improve functional grouping and retrieve related graph context. The disposable index stays outside Git.
Visual QA node used to verify dense grap: Visual QA node used to verify dense graph layout, dark/light theme rendering, and sidebar scrolling.
WASD-style keys are reserved for 3D canv: WASD-style keys are reserved for 3D canvas camera navigation. Choose a different chord.
What should be changed before this can b: What should be changed before this can be accepted?
When Chat auto-approve is enabled, it al: When Chat auto-approve is enabled, it also applies graph deletions without showing a review card.
When enabled, commands that reference pa: When enabled, commands that reference paths outside the project root or additional allowed roots are blocked before execution.
When enabled, Gaia's AI Implement run pa: When enabled, Gaia's AI Implement run pauses after planning so you can approve or reject the plan before any coding phase starts.
When enabled, generated source diffs wai: When enabled, generated source diffs wait for your review before ArchiCode continues to verification.
When enabled, manual plan approval is bl: When enabled, manual plan approval is blocked until open planning questions are answered or dismissed. Turn it off to approve a plan while skipping those questions.
